# Kindex

> The memory layer AI coding agents do not have. A persistent knowledge graph for AI-assisted workflows, distributed as an MCP server and standalone CLI. Indexes conversations, projects, and intellectual work so Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Google Antigravity, OpenCode, Cursor, and other MCP-capable agents never start a session blind.

Kindex is a weighted knowledge graph (SQLite + FTS5 + graph traversal) that captures not just what happened but what it means and how it connects. It ships as a free MCP plugin for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Google Antigravity, OpenCode, Cursor, and other agents or a standalone `kin` CLI on PyPI.

## What Kindex is

- **MCP server**: `kin-mcp` exposes 52 tools to Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Google Antigravity, OpenCode, Cursor, and other MCP clients (search, add, edit, supersede, context, show, ask, learn, link, list_nodes, status, suggest, graph_stats, graph_heal, graph_merge, dream, changelog, ingest, tag_start, tag_update, tag_resume, task_add, task_done, task_list, task_claim, task_release, coord_start, coord_join, coord_post, coord_read, coord_attach, coord_inject, coord_list, coord_end, lock_acquire, lock_release, watch_add, watch_list, watch_resolve, remind_create, remind_list, remind_snooze, remind_done, remind_check, remind_exec, mode_activate, mode_list, mode_show, mode_create, mode_export, mode_import, mode_seed).
- **CLI**: 75 `kin` subcommands for scripts, cron, lifecycle integration, and humans.
- **Transport**: stdio (local). Package: `pip install kindex[mcp]`.
- **Version**: 0.25.0. License: MIT. Repo: https://github.com/jmcentire/kindex.
- **Claude Code setup**: `claude mcp add --scope user --transport stdio kindex -- kin-mcp`, `kin setup-claude-md --install`, and `kin setup-hooks`.
- **Codex setup**: `kin setup-codex-mcp`, `kin setup-codex-hooks`, and `kin setup-agents-md --install --global`.
- **Gemini CLI setup**: `kin setup-gemini-mcp` and `kin setup-gemini-md --install`.
- **Google Antigravity setup**: `kin setup-antigravity-mcp`, `kin setup-antigravity-hooks`, and `kin setup-antigravity-md --install`.
- **OpenCode setup**: `kin setup-opencode-mcp` and `kin setup-agents-md --install --global`.
- **Cursor setup**: `kin setup-cursor-mcp` and `kin setup-cursor-rules --install`.
- **Codex session ingest**: `kin ingest codex-sessions`.
- **Agent tuning**: `kin agent-config show` and `kin agent-config set <key> <value>` support global defaults, agent defaults, and per-instance overrides for attention cadence/budgets, simulation checks, collaboration prompt cooldown, and lifecycle priming behavior.
- **Project context**: `.kin/config`, `.kin/index.json`, and `.kin/code-map.json` are intended to ship with the repo. Local/private runtime state belongs in `~/.kindex` or ignored `.kin/local`, `.kin/cache`, `.kin/tmp`, `.kin/private`.
- **Portable artifacts**: tracked `.kin` files must be self-contained and machine-portable. Code-map paths are repo-relative POSIX paths. Do not commit local filesystem pointers, secrets, private transcripts, or local-only report references into tracked artifacts.
- **Work policy**: `kin policy check` reads opt-in policy from `.kin/config`. Linear enforcement only applies when `work_policy.linear.enabled: true`.

## AI operating contract

- Start meaningful work with `tag_start` or `tag_resume`, then `search`, `context`, or `ask`.
- Use `task_add`, `task_list`, and `task_done` for durable work items. Do not rely on host-session-only task state for work that must survive the current conversation.
- Search before `add`; prefer `edit` or `supersede` over duplicate nodes.
- Capture decisions, constraints, questions, watches, and notable outputs while they are fresh, then link related nodes.
- Treat tracked `.kin` files as shipped project state, not local cache.
- If a generated artifact matters to future users or agents, make it repo-local, durable, and independently usable after a fresh clone.
- If the host also exposes session-local task tools, use those only for temporary planning; durable tasks belong in Kindex.
- Use `kin export` for audience-aware graph export and `kin export code-map` for current repo structure, code dependencies, layers, and repo-relative file paths.

## Key capabilities

- Hybrid retrieval: FTS5 BM25 + graph BFS + optional vectors, merged via Reciprocal Rank Fusion.
- Five context tiers (full / abridged / summarized / executive / index) auto-selected by token budget -- your plugin does not eat the context window.
- Node types for operational guardrails: `constraint` (blocks deploys), `directive` (soft rules), `watch` (attention flags), `checkpoint` (pre-flight).
- Session tags track named work context across conversations. `tag start` / `tag update` / `tag segment` / `tag resume` / `tag end`.
- Reminders with actionable shell commands or headless `claude -p` invocations.
- Dream cycle: throttled post-session consolidation (fuzzy dedup, capped suggestion writes, suggestion auto-apply, edge strengthening).
- `.kin/` inheritance chains: repo inherits from team, team inherits from org voice; repo-local policy can require active tags, Linear IDs, or git gates only when the repo opts in.

- Agent skills: https://kindex.tools/.well-known/agent-skills/index.json
